attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- less-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- brand-voice:discover-brand: Search connected platforms for brand materials and produce a discovery report
- brand-voice:enforce-voice: Apply brand guidelines to content creation
- brand-voice:generate-guidelines: Generate brand voice guidelines from documents, transcripts, discovery reports, or any combination
- anthropic-skills:internal-comms: A set of resources to help me write all kinds of internal communications, using the formats that my company likes to use. Claude should use this skill whenever asked to write some sort of internal communications (status reports, leadership updates, 3P updates, company newsletters, FAQs, incident reports, project updates, etc.).
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:brand-guidelines: Applies Anthropic's official brand colors and typography to any sort of artifact that may benefit from having Anthropic's look-and-feel. Use it when brand colors or style guidelines, visual formatting, or company design standards apply.
- anthropic-skills:doc-coauthoring: Guide users through a structured workflow for co-authoring documentation. Use when user wants to write documentation, proposals, technical specs, decision docs, or similar structured content. This workflow helps users efficiently transfer context, refine content through iteration, and verify the doc works for readers. Trigger when user mentions writing docs, creating proposals, drafting specs, or similar documentation tasks.
- anthropic-skills:mcp-builder: Guide for creating high-quality MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers that enable LLMs to interact with external services through well-designed tools. Use when building MCP servers to integrate external APIs or services, whether in Python (FastMCP) or Node/TypeScript (MCP SDK).
- anthropic-skills:algorithmic-art: Creating algorithmic art using p5.js with seeded randomness and interactive parameter exploration. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. - anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. - anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. - anthropic-skills:theme-factory: Toolkit for styling artifacts with a theme. These artifacts can be slides, docs, reportings, HTML landing pages, etc. There are 10 pre-set themes with colors/fonts that you can apply to any artifact that has been creating, or can generate a new theme on-the-fly.

aiTitle: Audit and consolidate JVTO Master Map project

content.content:
1	# **JVTO MASTER MAP**
2	
3	## **1\. STRATEGY**
4	
5	| Layer | Pin Point | Meaning for JVTO | Strategic Objective |
6	| ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- |
7	| Core Strategic Goal | Rebuild and organize the JVTO website systematically | The work is not only page redesign, but system building | Turn JVTO into a structured commercial and trustable website system |
8	| Commercial Goal | Make the website feel convincing and professional | The website must clearly show what JVTO sells and why it is credible | Increase booking confidence and reduce hesitation |
9	| Human Trust Goal | Make JVTO easier to trust for real users | Users must understand the operator, the people behind it, and the operational seriousness | Build human trust through clarity and proof |
10	| Machine Trust Goal | Make JVTO readable and credible for AI/search | The website must communicate that JVTO is a real entity with real proof, real founder, real updates, and real processes | Build machine-readable authority and trust |
11	| System Goal | Unite all pages into one coherent system | Pages should not feel isolated or disconnected | Build one consistent architecture across all core clusters |
12	| Source-of-Truth Goal | Use SSOT as the source of truth | Content placement and role must come from mapped source logic | Prevent scattered content and duplication |
13	| Package Strategy | Package-first self-service booking clarity | Package pages must help users decide without repeated manual questions | Make package pages function as self-service brochures |
14	| Trust Strategy | Guardian-grade trust infrastructure | Trust must be visible, verifiable, and structured | Strengthen authority, safety, and proof logic |
15	| Support Strategy | Support should reduce friction, not take over the flow | Support pages help users proceed, not distract them from booking | Keep support available without replacing package flow |
16	| Final Strategic Synthesis | Guardian-grade trust infrastructure \+ package-first self-service booking clarity | This is the combined direction extracted from the material | Position JVTO as a verifiable private volcano operator with clear, bookable products |
17	
18	---
19	
20	## **2\. STRUCTURE**
21	
22	| Layer | Pin Point | Meaning for JVTO | Structural Role |
23	| ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- |
24	| Homepage | Main commercial front door | Entry point for trust and initial discovery | Opens trust and directs next action |
25	| Tours | Commercial route path | Main path for package discovery and selection | Helps users choose packages |
26	| Destinations | Place-context path | Gives destination context and supports discovery | Supports selection with destination understanding |
27	| Package Pages | Product decision pages | Must present package logic in a structured order | Close the booking decision |
28	| Verify JVTO | Proof center | Owner for legal, medical, safety, press, and partner proof | Central proof ownership layer |
29	| Travel Guide | Operational readiness center | Owner for preparation, route realities, closures, and readiness | Practical support layer |
30	| Why JVTO | Story, team, and trust center | Owner for narrative differentiation and people/trust framing | Human trust and brand rationale layer |
31	| Policy | Formal rules layer | Owner for payment, cancellation, and formal rules | Booking rules support |
32	| Contact | Human escalation path | Backup help path, not first-line explanation | Final support escalation |
33	| ISIC | Existing sub-cluster already brought into the system | Part of the broader trust/offer consistency work | Related support or offer cluster |
34	
35	### **Core Page Relationship**
36	
37	| Flow Stage | Page / Cluster | Function |
38	| ----- | ----- | ----- |
39	| 1 | Homepage | Trust-first entry |
40	| 2 | Tours / Destinations | Discovery and route selection |
41	| 3 | Package Pages | Self-service conversion and practical clarity |
42	| 4 | Support / Verify / Policy | Confirmation, proof, and hesitation reduction |
43	| 5 | Contact | Human escalation if needed |
44	
45	### **Homepage Structure Logic**
46	
47	| Homepage Question | Required Structural Answer |
48	| ----- | ----- |
49	| What is this website about? | Hero, H1, subcopy |
50	| Why should I trust it? | Trust strip, reviews, founder context, doctor/Ijen proof, MAGMA/live context |
51	| What can I choose? | Route selector, destination cards, package cards |
52	| What should I do next? | CTA, contact, verify, explore tours |
53	
54	### **Package Page Structure Logic**
55	
56	| Final Package Flow Mentioned in Source | Structural Purpose |
57	| ----- | ----- |
58	| Hero package | Immediate product framing |
59	| Structured route data | Core trip facts |
60	| Gallery / visual route | Visual reinforcement |
61	| Route fit | Suitability guidance |
62	| Route rhythm | Journey pacing explanation |
63	| Hotel / rooming | Accommodation clarity |
64	| Vehicle / crew | Operational handling clarity |
65	| Meals | Inclusion clarity |
66	| Ijen readiness if relevant | Specific route readiness |
67	| Compact policy | Rule clarity without overwhelming flow |
68	| Payment summary | Payment clarity |
69	| Add-ons | Controlled upsell / extra options |
70	| Closest alternative | Route comparison / fallback support |
71	| Ijen proof rail if relevant | Route-specific proof layer |
72	| Verify-before-book | Trust confirmation |
73	| FAQ | Repeated friction reduction |
74	| Final CTA | Booking direction |
75	
76	### **Structural Principles**
77	
78	| Principle | Meaning |
79	| ----- | ----- |
80	| Package-first | Product comes first |
81	| Practicalities next | Important operational details appear early |
82	| Trust later | Heavy trust/proof supports the decision rather than leading the page |
83	| Support without overload | Support links should not interrupt the main package flow |
84	| Global trust architecture remains intact | Package-page refinement must not damage the wider trust system |
85	
86	---
87	
88	## **3\. VISUAL**
89	
90	| Layer | Pin Point | Meaning for JVTO | Visual Direction |
91	| ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- |
92	| Visual Goal | Make the site feel more convincing and professional | Visuals must support credibility, not just aesthetics | Stronger perception of operator seriousness |
93	| Current Visual State | More consistent than before | Typography, color, and rhythm are more aligned already | Foundation exists, but not fully locked |
94	| Working Visual System | Navy primary, orange action accent, lime/bright verification accent | A usable color system already exists | Base design system is partially in place |
95	| Current Visual Work Type | Frontend visual redesign | The work covers layout rhythm, section styling, cards, hero, CTA style, and image treatment | Implementation-level visual alignment |
96	| Not Yet Locked | Final art direction | Visual identity still needs stronger refinement and clarity | Aesthetic direction is not final yet |
97	| Suitable Art Direction | Premium expedition | Supports the travel \+ authority mix | Core aesthetic direction |
98	| Suitable Art Direction | Trust-led tour operator | Keeps trust central without making the site feel bureaucratic | Brand behavior mode |
99	| Suitable Art Direction | Volcanic travel with operational authority | Matches Bromo/Ijen and field seriousness | Final combined visual identity |
100	
101	### **What the Visual Direction Must Support**
102	
103	| Visual Requirement | Meaning |
104	| ----- | ----- |
105	| Travel-first feel | The site must still feel like a travel operator website |
106	| Trustable tone | It must feel serious and reliable |
107	| Mature and calm layout | Avoid noisy or juvenile interface behavior |
108	| Premium \+ expedition balance | Avoid both cheap travel-blog feel and sterile corporate feel |
109	| Scan-friendly content blocks | Visuals must support practical decision-making |
110	| Operational honesty | Design must not hide real operational facts |
111	
112	### **What Is Considered Suitable Reference Direction**
113	
114	| Suitable Reference Criteria | Meaning |
115	| ----- | ----- |
116	| Feels like a tour operator, not a blog | Commercial fit |
117	| Image-led | Travel-first visual cue |
118	| Short but firm text | Readable and decisive |
119	| Trust near decisions | Conversion-aligned trust |
120	| Mature, calm layout | Premium adult tone |
121	| Supports reviews, doctor proof, MAGMA, founder | Compatible with JVTO trust architecture |
122	
123	### **What Is Considered Unsuitable**
124	
125	| Unsuitable Reference Criteria | Meaning |
126	| ----- | ----- |
127	| Too SaaS/dashboard-like | Wrong category language |
128	| Too playful / colorful | Too light for JVTO authority |
129	| Too editorial / magazine-like | Weak commercial clarity |
130	| Too hotel / ultra luxury resort-like | Wrong product identity |
131	| Too corporate sterile | Loses travel character |
132	| Too icon-heavy | Visual noise |
133	| Too much dark overlay | Can feel too heavy and non-travel |
134	| Too much copy | Hurts scanability |
135	| Too abstract / not bookable | Weak conversion support |
136	
137	### **Homepage Component Visual Reference Priorities Mentioned**
138	
139	| Priority | Component |
140	| ----- | ----- |
141	| 1 | Hero |
142	| 2 | Destination cards |
143	| 3 | Package cards |
144	| 4 | Trust block |
145	| 5 | Founder spotlight |
146	| 6 | Final CTA |
147	| 7 | Footer |
148	
149	---
150	
151	## **4\. DATA**
152	
153	| Layer | Pin Point | Meaning for JVTO | Data Role |
154	| ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- |
155	| SSOT | Source of truth | SSOT content is mapped, not pasted blindly | Governs placement and ownership |
156	| Content Placement Mapping | Assets are mapped to homepage, verify, travel guide, people/team pages | Each content type needs a clear destination | Prevents scattering and overlap |
157	| Media Registry | SSOT already mapped into placement and media registry | Asset organization is already partly structured | Supports consistent content control |
158	| MAGMA Bromo & Ijen | Integrated as live context | Real-time or live operational context is part of trust | Freshness and operational data layer |
159	| Ijen Health Screening | Strengthened with real doctor context | Medical workflow is treated as real operational data | Route-specific trust/support data |
160	| Founder / Team / Reviews / Legal / Partner / Press | Already brought into the trust system | Trust graph data has already been structurally connected | Authority and proof layer |
161	| Local TS Files | Still a major current source in practice | CMS/PostgreSQL is not yet the primary source | Current implementation still relies on local structured files |
162	| Future CMS/PostgreSQL | Planned as the stronger long-term data layer | Needed for content updates, route data, trust asset management, and durability | Next major data-layer step |
163	
164	### **Data Domains Mentioned in the Material**
165	
166	| Data Domain | Description |
167	| ----- | ----- |
168	| Commercial content | Package, route, destination, and homepage content |
169	| Trust content | Founder, team, reviews, legal proof, partner context, press |
170	| Operational content | Travel guide, Ijen health screening, route readiness, closures |
171	| Live operational signals | MAGMA Bromo and Ijen |
172	| Policy content | Formal booking/payment/cancellation rules |
173	| Media assets | Hero images, route cards, proof screenshots, founder/team photos |
174	
175	### **Data Issues / Gaps Still Open**
176	
177	| Data Gap | Meaning |
178	| ----- | ----- |
179	| CMS/PostgreSQL not yet primary | Data management is not yet fully centralized |
180	| Media curation not final | Stronger asset selection is still needed |
181	| Copy not fully finalized | Content still needs editorial refinement |
182	| Domain routing not final | Production exposure is not fully finalized |
183	
184	---
185	
186	**Strategic Framework: Transitioning to a Trust-First Architecture for JVTO**
187	
188	## **1\. The Strategic Pivot: From Aesthetic Landing to Trust-Led Entry**
189	
190	In the high-stakes sector of specialized expedition tourism, visual allure is no longer the primary driver of conversion; trust is. For JVTO, the strategic pivot requires a fundamental shift from a traditional, aesthetic-heavy landing page to a "Trust-First" architecture. In environments involving significant physical demands and operational complexities, the user’s primary friction point is skepticism. By architecting the entry point around verified authority and operational transparency, JVTO establishes a critical competitive differentiator that separates professional operators from mere booking intermediaries.
191	
192	The table below defines how the core architecture must prioritize trust, discovery, and conversion to align with the user’s mental model:
193	
194	| Category | Strategic Pin Point | Meaning |
195	| :---- | :---- | :---- |
196	| **Trust Entry** | Open with Trust First | Immediate perception of JVTO as a serious, proof-backed operator rather than a visual gallery. |
197	| **Route Discovery** | Guided Trip Pathing | Navigation designed to prevent choice paralysis by offering clear entries based on origin or destination. |
198	| **Package Conversion** | Closing the Decision | A standalone environment that moves the user from interest to confidence through modular, granular data. |
199	
200	### **Analysis: Trust Above the Fold**
201	
202	The "So What?" layer of the homepage must address subconscious safety and legitimacy concerns instantly. Positioning trust blocks—specifically the "Tourist Police-led" status and private-only operation markers—above the fold is a functional mandate to reduce bounce rates. For serious travel routes, initial engagement is contingent upon the platform’s ability to project competence. When the system leads with "Trust Above the Fold," it provides the psychological permission necessary for the user to commit cognitive resources to the selection process.
203	
204	This established trust necessitates a logical, frictionless flow into the specific mechanics of route selection and discovery.
205	
206	\--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
207	
208	## **2\. The Taxonomy of Influence: Content Clusters and Hierarchy**
209	
210	A sophisticated information architecture must be clustered by user intent rather than internal administrative logic. This approach transforms the website into a friction reduction system, guiding the user through a journey of progressive clarification.
211	
212	### **The Four Primary Content Clusters**
213	
214	* **Discovery Cluster:** **Goal: Simplify Selection.** Logic: Prevent decision fatigue by grouping tours by origin (Surabaya/Bali), duration, and destination.  
215	* **Trust Cluster:** **Goal: Establish Authority.** Logic: Govern the narrative of "Who" and "Why" through differentiation and the expertise of the founder and crew.  
216	* **Support Cluster:** **Goal: Reduce Friction.** Logic: A "Prepare & Book" system providing field realities such as weather, medical screenings, and fitness requirements.  
217	* **Proof Cluster:** **Goal: Enable Auditing.** Logic: Centralize legal, institutional, and social evidence into a single, high-authority "Verify" repository.
218	
219	### **Content Hierarchy (Levels 1–6)**
220	
221	To move the user from initial differentiation to booking action, the system must present information in the following prioritized sequence:
222	
223	1. **Differentiation:** Why JVTO is unique (e.g., Police-led, private-only).  
224	2. **Fit:** Immediate identification of which specific route matches the user’s profile.  
225	3. **Experience:** The "rhythm" of the trip—terrain, tempo, and overnight logic.  
226	4. **Operational Reality:** The mechanics—hotel logic, vehicle types, pickup flow, and crew handling.  
227	5. **Verification:** The path to hard proof (Verify JVTO center).  
228	6. **Action:** Frictionless booking and inquiry triggers.
229	
230	This hierarchy ensures that broad brand authority effectively transitions into the granular mechanics of the Discovery Cluster.
231	
232	\--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
233	
234	## **3\. The Discovery Cluster: Optimizing the Selection Journey**
235	
236	Route discovery must be architected to prevent decision fatigue. The Discovery Cluster functions as a tool for progressive clarification, ensuring the user is never overwhelmed by unstructured choice.
237	
238	### **Logic for Route Selection**
239	
240	* **Origin-Based Entry:** Clear navigational paths for Surabaya vs. Bali, acknowledging the user's existing logistical constraints.  
241	* **Route Seriousness System:** A proprietary UI element that uses intensity and terrain cues to signal physical demands, ensuring fit clarity.  
242	* **Package Card Differentiators:** Standardized cards displaying duration, destination, and specialized labels (e.g., "Photography-optimized" or "Family-ready").
243	
244	### **Clarity-Led vs. Aesthetic-Led Models**
245	
246	The proposed architecture aggressively departs from the "aesthetic-led" model—which relies on vague imagery—in favor of a "clarity-led" framework. In this model, proprietary UI labels and filters (duration, seriousness, destination) serve as functional tools rather than mere decorations. By prioritizing clarity, the system ensures that by the time a user selects a route, they have already performed a preliminary self-screening. Consequently, the burden of proof shifts from the general brand to the specific package page.
247	
248	\--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
249	
250	## **4\. The Package Page: Architecture of Confidence and Conversion**
251	
252	The Package Page acts as the "Brochure \+ Conversion" layer. It is a standalone decision-making environment where all operational, trust, and logistical data must converge to finalize the booking.
253	
254	### **Fixed Modular Structure for Site Developers**
255	
256	To ensure consistency and maximum conversion, every package page must follow this mandatory modular sequence:
257	
258	* **Route Fit:** Specific definition of the ideal traveler profile.  
259	* **Itinerary Rhythm:** A granular breakdown of tempo and daily experience.  
260	* **Operational Reality:** Explicit details on **Hotel logic**, **Vehicle type**, and **Crew expertise**.  
261	* **Inclusions & Support:** Direct integration of the Ijen Health Screening and fitness requirements.  
262	* **Integrated Trust Blocks:** Snapshot reviews and safety credentials specific to the route.
263	
264	### **Integrating Operational Realities**
265	
266	The inclusion of field realities—such as weather uncertainties, medical screening, and physical fitness demands—is not a deterrent; it is a trust-builder. Architecting these "serious operational processes" directly into the package page demonstrates professional preparedness. This transparency transforms potential points of hesitation into evidence of expertise. Should a user require further validation, the architecture must provide a direct path to the Proof Ownership layer.
267	
268	\--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
269	
270	## **5\. The Trust Graph: Verification, Authority, and Proof Ownership**
271	
272	Trust is most effective when centralized and auditable. The "Verify JVTO" center serves as the platform's anchor, housing hard proof that validates narrative claims.
273	
274	### **Trust & Authority Asset Categories**
275	
276	* **Human Trust:** Faces and expertise, including the Founder's authority and a crew registry linked to field competence.  
277	* **Institutional Trust:** Formal legitimacy evidenced by legal status and specific partnerships with **ISIC, HPWKI, and INDECON**.  
278	* **Social Trust:** Grouped reviews categorized by strength (e.g., safety, photography quality) rather than simple chronological order.
279	
280	### **Differentiation: "Why JVTO" vs. "Verify JVTO"**
281	
282	The system must maintain a sharp distinction between these two entities. **Why JVTO** is the narrative layer focused on competitive differentiation and storytelling. **Verify JVTO** is the audit layer focused on proof, medical protocols (Ijen Screening), and legal credentials. Keeping these separate ensures that the narrative does not dilute the audit. Technically, these trust signals must be optimized for both human visitors and AI/GEO search crawlers to establish machine-readable authority.
283	
284	\--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
285	
286	## **6\. Operational Infrastructure: The Support and Technical Layer**
287	
288	The Support Cluster must function as an "Intent-Based Support" system designed to reduce friction at the point of booking.
289	
290	### **Booking Support Action Items**
291	
292	High-priority transparency tools are required to eliminate pre-booking anxiety:
293	
294	* **Ijen Health Screening:** Presented as a serious medical verification process.  
295	* **Weather & Closures:** Real-time field condition updates.  
296	* **Operational Flow:** Absolute clarity on pickup, transport, and payment (deposit/cancellation).
297	
298	### **Technical Search Layer Requirements**
299	
300	To ensure AI/GEO search visibility and technical authority, the following mandates must be implemented:
301	
302	* **Schema & JSON-LD:** Machine-readable data identifying the founder, organization, and route-specific entities.  
303	* **Canonical Tags:** Established ownership for every route and page cluster to prevent ambiguity.  
304	* **Alt Text Standardization:** Purposeful descriptions supporting accessibility and image-based search authority.  
305	* **Internal Trust Paths:** Strategic linking rules connecting package conversion points to the "Verify" center.
306	
307	These technical steps provide the foundation for future-proofing the platform’s digital authority.
308	
309	\--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
310	
311	## **7\. Implementation Roadmap: Execution Priorities and Success Conditions**
312	
313	The execution follows an "Improve-in-Place" methodology, systematically upgrading the platform while protecting the live data foundation.
314	
315	### **Fixed Priority Order**
316	
317	1. **Priority 1: Package Pages:** Audit and implement the fixed modular structure and operational details.  
318	2. **Priority 2: Tours Hub:** Refine origin-based grouping and the Route Seriousness System.  
319	3. **Priority 3: Homepage:** Re-orchestrate the entry logic to prioritize trust and discovery blocks.  
320	4. **Priority 4: Trust/Support Cluster:** Formalize the "Verify JVTO" center and institutional proof.  
321	5. **Priority 5: Technical Search Layer:** Deploy Schema, JSON-LD, Canonical tags, and Alt Text.  
322	6. **Priority 6: CMS/Content System:** Finalize field logic and scalable content models.  
323	7. **Priority 7: Future Expansion:** Introduce destination guides and thematic collections only after the foundation is stable.
324	
325	### **Success Conditions Mapping**
326	
327	| Category | Target Outcome | Action Items for Implementation |
328	| :---- | :---- | :---- |
329	| **Differentiation** | Immediate Distinction | Surface "Tourist Police-led" and "Private-only" status on homepage/cards. |
330	| **Fit Clarity** | Reduced Cognitive Load | Implement Route Seriousness UI and origin-based hub grouping. |
331	| **Proof Visibility** | Auditability | Centralize **ISIC, HPWKI, and INDECON** credentials in Verify JVTO. |
332	| **Booking Confidence** | High Conversion | Integrate medical screening and operational flow into package pages. |
333	
334	This framework transforms JVTO from a standard travel platform into a high-trust authority system, converting discerning travelers through an uncompromising architecture of operational transparency and verified proof.
335

# **JVTO MASTER MAP**

## **1\. STRATEGY**

| Layer | Pin Point | Meaning for JVTO | Strategic Objective |
| ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- |
| Core Strategic Goal | Rebuild and organize the JVTO website systematically | The work is not only page redesign, but system building | Turn JVTO into a structured commercial and trustable website system |
| Commercial Goal | Make the website feel convincing and professional | The website must clearly show what JVTO sells and why it is credible | Increase booking confidence and reduce hesitation |
| Human Trust Goal | Make JVTO easier to trust for real users | Users must understand the operator, the people behind it, and the operational seriousness | Build human trust through clarity and proof |
| Machine Trust Goal | Make JVTO readable and credible for AI/search | The website must communicate that JVTO is a real entity with real proof, real founder, real updates, and real processes | Build machine-readable authority and trust |
| System Goal | Unite all pages into one coherent system | Pages should not feel isolated or disconnected | Build one consistent architecture across all core clusters |
| Source-of-Truth Goal | Use SSOT as the source of truth | Content placement and role must come from mapped source logic | Prevent scattered content and duplication |
| Package Strategy | Package-first self-service booking clarity | Package pages must help users decide without repeated manual questions | Make package pages function as self-service brochures |
| Trust Strategy | Guardian-grade trust infrastructure | Trust must be visible, verifiable, and structured | Strengthen authority, safety, and proof logic |
| Support Strategy | Support should reduce friction, not take over the flow | Support pages help users proceed, not distract them from booking | Keep support available without replacing package flow |
| Final Strategic Synthesis | Guardian-grade trust infrastructure \+ package-first self-service booking clarity | This is the combined direction extracted from the material | Position JVTO as a verifiable private volcano operator with clear, bookable products |

---

## **2\. STRUCTURE**

| Layer | Pin Point | Meaning for JVTO | Structural Role |
| ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- |
| Homepage | Main commercial front door | Entry point for trust and initial discovery | Opens trust and directs next action |
| Tours | Commercial route path | Main path for package discovery and selection | Helps users choose packages |
| Destinations | Place-context path | Gives destination context and supports discovery | Supports selection with destination understanding |
| Package Pages | Product decision pages | Must present package logic in a structured order | Close the booking decision |
| Verify JVTO | Proof center | Owner for legal, medical, safety, press, and partner proof | Central proof ownership layer |
| Travel Guide | Operational readiness center | Owner for preparation, route realities, closures, and readiness | Practical support layer |
| Why JVTO | Story, team, and trust center | Owner for narrative differentiation and people/trust framing | Human trust and brand rationale layer |
| Policy | Formal rules layer | Owner for payment, cancellation, and formal rules | Booking rules support |
| Contact | Human escalation path | Backup help path, not first-line explanation | Final support escalation |
| ISIC | Existing sub-cluster already brought into the system | Part of the broader trust/offer consistency work | Related support or offer cluster |

### **Core Page Relationship**

| Flow Stage | Page / Cluster | Function |
| ----- | ----- | ----- |
| 1 | Homepage | Trust-first entry |
| 2 | Tours / Destinations | Discovery and route selection |
| 3 | Package Pages | Self-service conversion and practical clarity |
| 4 | Support / Verify / Policy | Confirmation, proof, and hesitation reduction |
| 5 | Contact | Human escalation if needed |

### **Homepage Structure Logic**

| Homepage Question | Required Structural Answer |
| ----- | ----- |
| What is this website about? | Hero, H1, subcopy |
| Why should I trust it? | Trust strip, reviews, founder context, doctor/Ijen proof, MAGMA/live context |
| What can I choose? | Route selector, destination cards, package cards |
| What should I do next? | CTA, contact, verify, explore tours |

### **Package Page Structure Logic**

| Final Package Flow Mentioned in Source | Structural Purpose |
| ----- | ----- |
| Hero package | Immediate product framing |
| Structured route data | Core trip facts |
| Gallery / visual route | Visual reinforcement |
| Route fit | Suitability guidance |
| Route rhythm | Journey pacing explanation |
| Hotel / rooming | Accommodation clarity |
| Vehicle / crew | Operational handling clarity |
| Meals | Inclusion clarity |
| Ijen readiness if relevant | Specific route readiness |
| Compact policy | Rule clarity without overwhelming flow |
| Payment summary | Payment clarity |
| Add-ons | Controlled upsell / extra options |
| Closest alternative | Route comparison / fallback support |
| Ijen proof rail if relevant | Route-specific proof layer |
| Verify-before-book | Trust confirmation |
| FAQ | Repeated friction reduction |
| Final CTA | Booking direction |

### **Structural Principles**

| Principle | Meaning |
| ----- | ----- |
| Package-first | Product comes first |
| Practicalities next | Important operational details appear early |
| Trust later | Heavy trust/proof supports the decision rather than leading the page |
| Support without overload | Support links should not interrupt the main package flow |
| Global trust architecture remains intact | Package-page refinement must not damage the wider trust system |

---

## **3\. VISUAL**

| Layer | Pin Point | Meaning for JVTO | Visual Direction |
| ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- |
| Visual Goal | Make the site feel more convincing and professional | Visuals must support credibility, not just aesthetics | Stronger perception of operator seriousness |
| Current Visual State | More consistent than before | Typography, color, and rhythm are more aligned already | Foundation exists, but not fully locked |
| Working Visual System | Navy primary, orange action accent, lime/bright verification accent | A usable color system already exists | Base design system is partially in place |
| Current Visual Work Type | Frontend visual redesign | The work covers layout rhythm, section styling, cards, hero, CTA style, and image treatment | Implementation-level visual alignment |
| Not Yet Locked | Final art direction | Visual identity still needs stronger refinement and clarity | Aesthetic direction is not final yet |
| Suitable Art Direction | Premium expedition | Supports the travel \+ authority mix | Core aesthetic direction |
| Suitable Art Direction | Trust-led tour operator | Keeps trust central without making the site feel bureaucratic | Brand behavior mode |
| Suitable Art Direction | Volcanic travel with operational authority | Matches Bromo/Ijen and field seriousness | Final combined visual identity |

### **What the Visual Direction Must Support**

| Visual Requirement | Meaning |
| ----- | ----- |
| Travel-first feel | The site must still feel like a travel operator website |
| Trustable tone | It must feel serious and reliable |
| Mature and calm layout | Avoid noisy or juvenile interface behavior |
| Premium \+ expedition balance | Avoid both cheap travel-blog feel and sterile corporate feel |
| Scan-friendly content blocks | Visuals must support practical decision-making |
| Operational honesty | Design must not hide real operational facts |

### **What Is Considered Suitable Reference Direction**

| Suitable Reference Criteria | Meaning |
| ----- | ----- |
| Feels like a tour operator, not a blog | Commercial fit |
| Image-led | Travel-first visual cue |
| Short but firm text | Readable and decisive |
| Trust near decisions | Conversion-aligned trust |
| Mature, calm layout | Premium adult tone |
| Supports reviews, doctor proof, MAGMA, founder | Compatible with JVTO trust architecture |

### **What Is Considered Unsuitable**

| Unsuitable Reference Criteria | Meaning |
| ----- | ----- |
| Too SaaS/dashboard-like | Wrong category language |
| Too playful / colorful | Too light for JVTO authority |
| Too editorial / magazine-like | Weak commercial clarity |
| Too hotel / ultra luxury resort-like | Wrong product identity |
| Too corporate sterile | Loses travel character |
| Too icon-heavy | Visual noise |
| Too much dark overlay | Can feel too heavy and non-travel |
| Too much copy | Hurts scanability |
| Too abstract / not bookable | Weak conversion support |

### **Homepage Component Visual Reference Priorities Mentioned**

| Priority | Component |
| ----- | ----- |
| 1 | Hero |
| 2 | Destination cards |
| 3 | Package cards |
| 4 | Trust block |
| 5 | Founder spotlight |
| 6 | Final CTA |
| 7 | Footer |

---

## **4\. DATA**

| Layer | Pin Point | Meaning for JVTO | Data Role |
| ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- |
| SSOT | Source of truth | SSOT content is mapped, not pasted blindly | Governs placement and ownership |
| Content Placement Mapping | Assets are mapped to homepage, verify, travel guide, people/team pages | Each content type needs a clear destination | Prevents scattering and overlap |
| Media Registry | SSOT already mapped into placement and media registry | Asset organization is already partly structured | Supports consistent content control |
| MAGMA Bromo & Ijen | Integrated as live context | Real-time or live operational context is part of trust | Freshness and operational data layer |
| Ijen Health Screening | Strengthened with real doctor context | Medical workflow is treated as real operational data | Route-specific trust/support data |
| Founder / Team / Reviews / Legal / Partner / Press | Already brought into the trust system | Trust graph data has already been structurally connected | Authority and proof layer |
| Local TS Files | Still a major current source in practice | CMS/PostgreSQL is not yet the primary source | Current implementation still relies on local structured files |
| Future CMS/PostgreSQL | Planned as the stronger long-term data layer | Needed for content updates, route data, trust asset management, and durability | Next major data-layer step |

### **Data Domains Mentioned in the Material**

| Data Domain | Description |
| ----- | ----- |
| Commercial content | Package, route, destination, and homepage content |
| Trust content | Founder, team, reviews, legal proof, partner context, press |
| Operational content | Travel guide, Ijen health screening, route readiness, closures |
| Live operational signals | MAGMA Bromo and Ijen |
| Policy content | Formal booking/payment/cancellation rules |
| Media assets | Hero images, route cards, proof screenshots, founder/team photos |

### **Data Issues / Gaps Still Open**

| Data Gap | Meaning |
| ----- | ----- |
| CMS/PostgreSQL not yet primary | Data management is not yet fully centralized |
| Media curation not final | Stronger asset selection is still needed |
| Copy not fully finalized | Content still needs editorial refinement |
| Domain routing not final | Production exposure is not fully finalized |

### **Technical Search Layer Requirements**

To ensure AI/GEO search visibility and technical authority, the following mandates must be implemented:

* **Schema & JSON-LD:** Machine-readable data identifying the founder, organization, and route-specific entities.  
* **Canonical Tags:** Established ownership for every route and page cluster to prevent ambiguity.  
* **Alt Text Standardization:** Purposeful descriptions supporting accessibility and image-based search authority.  
* **Internal Trust Paths:** Strategic linking rules connecting package conversion points to the "Verify" center.

These technical steps provide the foundation for future-proofing the platform’s digital authority.

\--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **7\. Implementation Roadmap: Execution Priorities and Success Conditions**

The execution follows an "Improve-in-Place" methodology, systematically upgrading the platform while protecting the live data foundation.

### **Fixed Priority Order**

1. **Priority 1: Package Pages:** Audit and implement the fixed modular structure and operational details.  
2. **Priority 2: Tours Hub:** Refine origin-based grouping and the Route Seriousness System.  
3. **Priority 3: Homepage:** Re-orchestrate the entry logic to prioritize trust and discovery blocks.  
4. **Priority 4: Trust/Support Cluster:** Formalize the "Verify JVTO" center and institutional proof.  
5. **Priority 5: Technical Search Layer:** Deploy Schema, JSON-LD, Canonical tags, and Alt Text.  
6. **Priority 6: CMS/Content System:** Finalize field logic and scalable content models.  
7. **Priority 7: Future Expansion:** Introduce destination guides and thematic collections only after the foundation is stable.

### **Success Conditions Mapping**

| Category | Target Outcome | Action Items for Implementation |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **Differentiation** | Immediate Distinction | Surface "Tourist Police-led" and "Private-only" status on homepage/cards. |
| **Fit Clarity** | Reduced Cognitive Load | Implement Route Seriousness UI and origin-based hub grouping. |
| **Proof Visibility** | Auditability | Centralize **ISIC, HPWKI, and INDECON** credentials in Verify JVTO. |
| **Booking Confidence** | High Conversion | Integrate medical screening and operational flow into package pages. |

This framework transforms JVTO from a standard travel platform into a high-trust authority system, converting discerning travelers through an uncompromising architecture of operational transparency and verified proof.

JVTO is being built as a commercial, trustable, structured, AI-readable website for PT Java Volcano Rendezvous (NIB: 1102230032918), a licensed Indonesian volcano tour operator led by Bripka Agung Sambuko (active Tourist Police, Ditpamobvit East Java).

**Why:** The goal is to build a verified digital entity readable by both humans and AI answer engines (YMYL category — active volcano tours trigger aggressive AI safety guardrails). Strategy is "agent-first" not just "SEO-first."

**How to apply:** All implementations must serve the 5-layer architecture (Trust Entry → Route Discovery → Package Conversion → Operational Support → Proof Ownership). See CLAUDE.md in project root for the full north star.

## Current Phase Status
- Phase A (Foundation) ✅ complete
- Phase B (Core Commercial — package pages, tours hub, homepage) 🔄 in progress
- Phase C (Technical Optimization — metadata, schema, AI-readable trust) ✅ complete (implemented 2026-04-14)
- Phase D (Content System / CMS) 📋 planned
- Phase E (Expansion — blog, guides) 📋 future

## What was implemented 2026-04-14
- `public/llms.txt` — high-density SSOT Markdown for LLM crawlers (OpenAI, Perplexity, Claude, Google AI)
- `public/.well-known/ai-agent-config.json` — machine-readable site map and trust signal handshake
- `components/JsonLd.tsx` — hardened with GovernmentPermit (NIB + HPWKI), MedicalWebPage (Ijen screening), GovernmentService (Tourist Police), Book citation (Stefan Loose ISBN-13: 978-3-7701-7881-0)
- `lib/verificationData.ts` — added FORENSIC_HASHES (SHA-256 for NIB and HPWKI documents)
- `public/robots.txt` — allows GPTBot, PerplexityBot, anthropic-ai, ClaudeBot; references llms.txt
- `CLAUDE.md` — project north star with full architecture, data sources, and working rules

## Key Data Files
- `lib/siteConfig.ts` — brand identity SSOT
- `lib/jvtoData.ts` — Tours, Destinations, Crew data
- `lib/verificationData.ts` — Legal, recognition, forensic proof data
- `lib/ssot-media-registry.ts` — media asset registry

---
# Database Analysis — jvto_dev (31.97.223.43)

**Connection ID:** `mcp://86C2EB80-3416-4ACB-84CC-BE06D3D07623/jvto_dev`

## Key Tables & Data Available

### 1. Tour Packages + Images
- **Table:** `packages` + `package_images`
- **Real Data:** 16+ tour packages with 5-6 unique images each
- **URL Format:** `/img/destinations/[timestamp]/[filename].webp`
- **Alt Text:** Each image has descriptive alt text (e.g., "Mount Bromo sunrise landscape", "Customer on horseback heading to Mount Bromo crater")
- **Issue Fixed:** Currently frontend hardcodes all tours to use same `home.webp` placeholder

**Example Package:**
- `bromo-2d1n` = "2 Day Bromo Sunrise Adventure from Surabaya"
- Has 6 images: Mount Bromo sunset, sunrise sequence, horseback riding, jeep at crater, etc.

### 2. Destinations + Hero Images
- **Table:** `destinations` + `destination_assets` + `assets`
- **Real Data:** 9 destinations with unique hero images
- **URL Format:** `/uploads/[timestamp]/[filename].jpg` or `.jpeg`
- **Available Destinations:** Mount Bromo, Ijen Crater, Madakaripura Waterfall, Tumpak Sewu Waterfall, Papuma Beach, Malang City, Bali, Surabaya, Taman Safari Prigen

### 3. Real Guest Testimonials
- **Table:** `reviews`
- **Real Data:** 10+ 5-star reviews from Trustpilot with actual guest names and detailed feedback
- **Columns:** customer_name, review (text), star (rating), platform, date, profile_photo

**Sample Real Reviews:**
- "Tan Yong Xue Jayden" (5⭐): "Our tour guide (KiKi) and driver (Nur) was amazing..."
- "Nina Kliem" (5⭐): "We did the 3D2N Bromo and Mount Ijen tour. It was organised very well..."
- "Patarachai Sereerat" (5⭐): "Had an amazing trip... special thanks to Yandi and Boy..."

### 4. Crew Members
- **Table:** `crew_members`, `crew_member_reviews`, `crew_roles`
- **Data:** Full crew information with roles, reviews, ratings

### 5. Activities & Itinerary
- **Table:** `package_itinerary_days`, `activities`, `activity_categories`
- **Data:** Day-by-day itinerary details, activity types and descriptions

### 6. Volcano/MAGMA Data
- **Table:** Likely available for live volcano status (need to explore further)

## Pain Points That Can Be Fixed

| Pain Point | Solution | Data Source |
|---|---|---|
| All 16 tours show same photo | Link each tour to its unique images via package_images table | `packages.slug` + `package_images.url` |
| All 5 destinations show same photo | Link each destination to its hero image | `destinations.slug` + `destination_assets` + `assets` |
| 4 fictional testimonials (Sarah Jenkins, etc.) | Replace with 8-10 real Trustpilot reviews | `reviews` table |
| Hardcoded volcano status (Level II Waspada) | Query MAGMA API or build stored procedure for live status | Need to explore further |
| 3 timeline photos reused | Get founder/historical photos from assets or archive | Need to explore further |

## Next Steps

1. **Create GraphQL/API endpoints** in Next.js to fetch:
   - Tour images by slug
   - Destination images by slug
   - Real testimonials (with caching)
   - Crew data for team page

2. **Update frontend components:**
   - `lib/jvtoData.ts` → Add imageUrl fields linking to database
   - `app/why-jvto/reviews/page.tsx` → Replace hardcoded testimonials with real DB data
   - `components/LiveSignalBlock.tsx` → Replace hardcoded status with API call

3. **Optional:** Build CMS endpoints for dynamic content management

## Connection Details

- **Host:** 31.97.223.43
- **Database:** jvto_dev
- **Profile ID:** 86C2EB80-3416-4ACB-84CC-BE06D3D07623
- **Total Tables:** 117
- **Schema:** PostgreSQL 13+

content.text:
Perfect. Now I have a comprehensive view. Let me create the audit report:

## JVTO Next.js Project Audit Report

### 1. Homepage Structure (`app/page.tsx`)

**Sections present (in order):**
1. TrustSummary (top banner)
2. Hero (with title, tagline, CTA, hero image)
3. DifferentiatorGrid (9-point value prop section)
4. Destinations section (featured cards: Mount Bromo, Ijen, etc.)
5. RouteSelector (interactive decision tool)
6. Ijen Health Proof Rail (clinic workflow block)
7. Trust Strip (5-pillar dark navy footer with NIB, police, private groups, ratings, permits)
8. TrustpilotWidget (4.9★ reviews, 112 verified)
9. MedicalTrustSection (health screening credentials)
10. FounderSpotlight (Agung Sambuko authority signal)
11. CrewGrid (14+ named crew members)
12. Trust Pillars section (Legal Identity, Police Context, Press & Guides, History)
13. 4x AnswerBlocks (Why trust JVTO, Ijen safety, private tours, cancellation)
14. Guest Reflections (3 review themes: Safety, Logistics, Guide care)
15. FAQSection (5 home FAQs)
16. Travel Guide CTA (Java Expedition Guide teaser)
17. ContactSection (inquiry form)
18. Verify JVTO lime bar CTA
19. Footer

**Assessment:** Follows strategic layout (Hero → Trust → Discovery → Details → Social proof → Founder → Crew → Pillars → Reviews → Guide → Contact → Footer). Deviates slightly in sequencing but covers all required trust signals comprehensively.

---

### 2. Core Homepage Components

**Exist and are fully built:**
- Hero (`components/Hero.tsx`) — full-bleed with image, rating, NIB, MAGMA status
- TrustStrip (inline at `app/page.tsx:203-233`) — 5 icons/labels
- RouteSelector (`components/RouteSelector.tsx`) — interactive tool
- DestinationCards (inline at `app/page.tsx:125-160`) — 4 featured destinations
- PackageCards (via TourCard component, used on tours hub)
- TrustBlock (inline as "Trust Pillars" section `app/page.tsx:261-319`)
- FounderSpotlight (`components/FounderSpotlight.tsx`)
- FinalCTA (inline ContactSection + Verify bar)
- DifferentiatorGrid (`components/DifferentiatorGrid.tsx`)
- TrustSummary (`components/TrustSummary.tsx`)

**Status:** All exist. None are stubs. All reference real data and images.

---

### 3. Tours Hub (`app/tours/page.tsx`)

**Implements:**
- Origin-based grouping: YES (lines 267-297: "Surabaya Hub" and "Bali Hub" links to `/tours/from-surabaya` and `/tours/from-bali`)
- Route Comparison table: YES (lines 133-179: Bromo Midnight vs Ijen Blue Fire vs Full Expedition with feature matrix)
- FeaturedTours: YES (lines 183-202: displays all tours via TourCard grid)
- Trust Standard section: YES (lines 204-227: "JVTO Standard" badge with Private/Licensed/Medical)
- Transparent Booking Logic: YES (lines 230-264: 3-step process + refund/reschedule box)

input.content:
# JVTO — Audit Konsolidasi & Roadmap Strategis

**Input:** `E:\Users\JAVA VOLCANO\Downloads\JVTO MASTER MAP.md` + kode aktual + memory project
**Tanggal audit:** 2026-04-19
**Branch:** main (clean)

---

## Context / Kenapa Ini Dibuat

User mengupload **JVTO MASTER MAP.md** (4 lapisan: Strategy, Structure, Visual, Data + Strategic Framework 7-seksi "Trust-First Architecture"). Master Map ini adalah ringkasan tertinggi dari arah strategis JVTO. Tugas: audit proyek aktual vs Master Map, konsolidasi temuan, identifikasi pain point, dan tetapkan langkah eksekusi yang realistis sampai tuntas.

Proyek JVTO sudah berada di tahap **Phase B (Core Commercial) berjalan** dan **Phase C (Technical Optimization) selesai**. Fondasinya kuat (30+ halaman, JSON-LD hardened, llms.txt, robots.txt), tetapi ada **kesenjangan antara klaim dokumen dan implementasi** yang jika tidak ditutup akan merusak seluruh narasi "trust-first, machine-readable, verifiable".

---

## 1. Korelasi Master Map ⇄ Kondisi Aktual

| Layer Master Map | Klaim Master Map | Kondisi Aktual Proyek | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Strategy – Guardian-grade trust + package-first** | 5-layer architecture (Trust Entry → Discovery → Package → Support → Proof) | Semua 5 layer ada, 30+ halaman lengkap | ✅ Aligned |
| **Structure – Homepage** | Hero → Trust → Discovery → CTA | 18 seksi berjalan di `app/page.tsx`, semua trust signals hadir | ✅ Aligned |
| **Structure – Tours hub** | Origin-based grouping + Route Seriousness System | Origin hubs (Surabaya/Bali) sudah ada; **"Route Seriousness" tidak eksplisit** (hanya implisit di field `physicality`) | ⚠ Partial |
| **Structure – Package page 17-modul** | Hero → … → Add-ons → Verify CTA → FAQ → Final CTA | **15/17 modul ada**; modul **Add-ons** dan **explicit Verify-before-book CTA** belum dirender | ⚠ Gap |
| **Visual – Navy/Orange/Lime + Raleway/Inter/JetBrains Mono** | Sistem warna + tipografi terkunci | Token CSS benar di `app/globals.css`; tidak ada hex hardcode di komponen | ✅ Aligned |
| **Data – SSOT + Media Registry** | `ssot-media-registry.ts` sebagai SSOT | **Tidak di-enforce** — 22 occurrence `home.webp` tersebar di 12 file pages bypass registry | ❌ Broken |
| **Data – MAGMA Bromo & Ijen live** | "Volcano status updated before every departure" (CLAUDE.md, llms.txt, ai-agent-config.json) | **LiveSignalBlock.tsx baris 14-29 HARDCODED** ("Level II Waspada", "Level I Normal"). `new Date()` hanya merender timestamp cek palsu. Tidak ada fetcher MAGMA. | ❌ Critical lie |
| **Data – PostgreSQL migration** | "Future CMS/PostgreSQL is planned" | **Sudah lebih jauh dari klaim**: DB `jvto_dev` live (22 packages), API routes `/api/tours`, `/api/destinations`, `/api/reviews/database`, Redis cache — **tetapi halaman masih import `lib/jvtoData.ts` statis (19 tours)** | ⚠ Dual-source |
| **Data – Reviews** | Trustpilot 4.9/112 + Guardian trust | `/why-jvto/reviews/page.tsx` sudah DB-backed dengan fallback; crew profiles punya `forensicReviewQuote` real. `siteConfig.aggregateRating` hardcoded 4.9/112 (lastUpdated 2026-03-01, stale). | ⚠ Partial |
| **Technical – JSON-LD + Schema** | TravelAgency, TouristTrip, Person, Book, MedicalWebPage | Semua sudah di `components/JsonLd.tsx` (1,309 LOC) | ✅ Aligned |
| **Technical – llms.txt + robots.txt + ai-agent-config** | AI/GEO readable | Semua file ada dan lengkap; `public/.well-known/ai-agent-config.json` live + API route duplicate | ✅ Aligned |

---

## 2. Pain Points (Tuntas-wajib, Urut Prioritas)

### P0 — Klaim tidak konsisten dengan implementasi (risiko trust & AI hallucination)

| # | Pain Point | Bukti | Dampak |
|---|---|---|---|
| **P0.1** | **MAGMA live claim vs data hardcoded** | `components/LiveSignalBlock.tsx:14-29` array statis. `public/llms.txt`, `CLAUDE.md`, `ai-agent-config.json` semua mengklaim live. | AI crawler akan menyimpulkan JVTO sumber data tidak akurat → kepercayaan entitas rusak. YMYL kategori (volcano tour) punya guardrail ketat. |
| **P0.2** | **`aggregateRating` hardcoded & stale** | `lib/siteConfig.ts` — 4.9/112 reviews, `lastUpdated 2026-03-01` (48 hari lalu) | Schema.org AggregateRating di JSON-LD dan halaman ikut stale. |
| **P0.3** | **Exchange rates stale** | `lib/siteConfig.ts` — USD 16250, EUR 17800, updated 2026-03-01 | Harga konversi salah → hesitation booking. |

### P1 — Arsitektur data dual-source (sumber kebenaran ambigu)

| # | Pain Point | Bukti | Dampak |
|---|---|---|---|
| **P1.1** | **`jvtoData.ts` (19 tours) ≠ DB (22 packages)** | `CONSOLIDATION_ANALYSIS.md` dokumentasi; kode: halaman masih `import { TOURS } from '@/lib/jvtoData'` bukan `fetch('/api/tours')` | 3 paket hilang dari site; inkonsistensi harga/image. |
| **P1.2** | **Image governance broken** | `home.webp` muncul 22x di 12 halaman (`app/travel-guide/page.tsx` 5x, `app/why-jvto/our-story/page.tsx` 3x, dll.). `ssot-media-registry.ts` tidak dipakai. | Banyak halaman menampilkan placeholder yang sama; AI agent akan flag sebagai "low-quality content". |
| **P1.3** | **`package_images` DB ada, belum tersambung ke UI** | Database punya 5-6 gambar unik per paket; `TourCard.tsx` pakai `tour.image` tunggal dari TS statis. | Gallery tour hanya 1 foto alih-alih 5-6. |

### P2 — Gap struktur (Master Map Structure belum 100%)

| # | Pain Point | Bukti | Dampak |
|---|---|---|---|
| **P2.1** | **Package page: 2/17 modul absen** | Tidak ada section **Add-ons** & **Verify-before-book CTA eksplisit** di `app/tours/[slug]/page.tsx` + `TourDetailClient.tsx` | Upsell hilang; jembatan ke `/verify-jvto` lemah di momen keputusan. |
| **P2.2** | **Route Seriousness System tidak eksplisit** | `app/tours/page.tsx` pakai `physicality` string bebas, bukan UI label berjenjang (Easy/Moderate/Serious/Expedition) | Discovery Cluster Master Map §3 tidak tercapai — user masih butuh baca copy untuk self-screen. |
| **P2.3** | **10 halaman tidak punya `generateMetadata`** | `/contact`, `/destinations`, `/policy/*`, `/tours/from-surabaya`, `/tours/from-bali` pakai metadata root template saja | Title/description generic → SEO & social preview buruk. |

### P3 — Technical debt yang menahan kecepatan

| # | Pain Point | Bukti | Dampak |
|---|---|---|---|
| **P3.1** | **TypeScript excludes diperluas** | Commits `7aadf51`, `6b4f8df` — test files + config files di-exclude dari TS build | Error test tidak terlihat saat build; regresi tidak ketangkap. |
| **P3.2** | **Firebase dead code** | `firebase.ts`, `firebase-*.json`, `firebase-tools` terinstall — **zero import** di kode page/component | Bundle size, confusing stack, security surface tidak perlu. |
| **P3.3** | **Folder `Redisgn Website JVTO`** (typo) kosong | 3 subfolder kosong: `destinations/`, `reviews/`, `tours/` | Artifact basi; bikin bingung. |
| **P3.4** | **Test coverage minim** | Hanya `Hero.test.tsx` + `TourCard.test.tsx` | Refactor berisiko silent-break. |
| **P3.5** | **Tidak ada `loading.tsx` / `error.tsx`** di route utama | Hanya `app/not-found.tsx` global | UX kosong saat fetch API lambat/gagal. |

---

## 3. Langkah Strategis (Roadmap Eksekusi, Urut Priority Fixed dari Master Map §7)

> **Metode:** Improve-in-Place. Jangan rebuild. Tutup gap dari P0 dulu (integritas klaim) → P1 (data truth) → P2 (UX struktur) → P3 (maintainability).

### SPRINT A — "No Lies" (Integritas Trust) — 1–2 hari kerja

**Goal:** Nol klaim bohong. Apa yang dibilang di CLAUDE.md, llms.txt, dan JSON-LD harus sama dengan yang di-render user/crawler.

1. **[P0.1] MAGMA live fetcher**
   - Buat `lib/magma.ts` — fetcher ke `https://magma.esdm.go.id/v1/...` (atau scrape resmi) untuk Bromo & Ijen
   - Buat `app/api/volcano/route.ts` — proxy + cache Redis (TTL 3600s, sudah ada `CACHE_TTL_VOLCANO` di `lib/cache.ts`)
   - `components/LiveSignalBlock.tsx` → async RSC atau client fetch ke `/api/volcano`; hapus array statis `VOLCANO_STATUSES`
   - **Kriteria selesai:** tampilan menunjukkan timestamp fetch nyata + data dari API; fallback graceful jika API down (tampil "Last verified: X, source: MAGMA" + link resmi)
   - **Jika MAGMA API bermasalah / tidak publik:** turunkan klaim di CLAUDE.md §7 & llms.txt menjadi "manual verification before each departure with reference link" — konsisten dengan kode

2. **[P0.2] Reviews aggregate dari DB**
   - Ganti `SITE_CONFIG.aggregateRating` statis → helper `getAggregateRating()` di `lib/siteConfig.ts` yang baca dari `/api/reviews/database` atau langsung `lib/db.ts`
   - `components/JsonLd.tsx` → AggregateRating schema baca dari helper ini di build/RSC time
   - **Kriteria selesai:** angka rating & jumlah review refleksi real-time dari PostgreSQL

3. **[P0.3] Exchange rates refresh**
   - Buat `scripts/refresh-exchange-rates.ts` + cron (via `scheduled-tasks` MCP atau Vercel cron) harian
   - Simpan ke Redis key `exchange_rates` + `exchange_rates_updated_at`; `siteConfig` helper baca dari cache
   - **Kriteria selesai:** harga konversi selalu < 24 jam umur

**Critical files:** `components/LiveSignalBlock.tsx`, `lib/siteConfig.ts`, `components/JsonLd.tsx`, `lib/cache.ts`, `app/api/volcano/route.ts` (baru), `lib/magma.ts` (baru)

---

### SPRINT B — "One Source of Truth" (Unifikasi Data) — 3–4 hari kerja

**Goal:** Halaman membaca dari PostgreSQL + API routes, bukan `lib/jvtoData.ts`. `jvtoData.ts` jadi fallback/seed saja.

1. **[P1.1] Migrate tour pages to API**
   - `app/tours/page.tsx`, `app/tours/[slug]/page.tsx`, `app/tours/from-surabaya/page.tsx`, `app/tours/from-bali/page.tsx` → ganti `import { TOURS } from '@/lib/jvtoData'` dengan `await fetch('/api/tours')` (RSC) atau helper `getTours()` di `lib/data-access.ts` yang baca DB langsung
   - `app/sitemap.ts` → baca dari DB supaya 22 packages (bukan 19) masuk
   - **Validation:** jalankan `npm run audit:prd` + bandingkan output dengan `/api/tours`; zero drift

2. **[P1.2] Enforce media registry**
   - Tambah util `lib/ssot-media-registry.ts::getHeroFor(zone, slug)` yang error di dev jika asset tidak terdaftar
   - Ganti semua 22 occurrence `home.webp` di `app/**/page.tsx` dengan panggilan registry
   - Tambah script `scripts/audit-media.ts` → CI gate yang fail kalau `home.webp` dipakai selain di `SITE_CONFIG.defaultHero`
   - **Kriteria selesai:** `grep -r "home.webp" app/` hanya muncul di registry, bukan di page code

3. **[P1.3] Wire package_images DB → gallery**
   - Perluas `/api/tours/[slug]` untuk return array `images[]` (sudah JSON agg di query SQL)
   - `components/TourCard.tsx` → pakai `images[0]` sebagai card; `TourDetailClient.tsx` gallery render semua 5-6 images
   - Migrasi Next `<Image>` dengan remotePatterns untuk `/uploads/` dan `/img/destinations/`

**Critical files:** `lib/data-access.ts` (baru), `app/tours/[slug]/page.tsx`, `components/TourCard.tsx`, `components/TourDetailClient.tsx`, `lib/ssot-media-registry.ts`, `next.config.mjs`, `scripts/audit-media.ts` (baru)

---

### SPRINT C — "Close the 17-Module Package Page" — 1–2 hari kerja

**Goal:** Halaman paket 100% modular sesuai Master Map §Package Page Structure Logic.

1. **[P2.1] Add-ons module**
   - Schema: tambah field `addOns: { id, label, priceIDR, description }[]` di `packages` DB (migration `npm run db:migrate`)
   - UI: `components/PackageAddOns.tsx` render checkbox + sub-total; posisi: setelah Payment summary, sebelum Closest Alternative
   - Seed data: isi untuk top-5 paket dulu (e.g., "Ijen medical clearance letter", "Extra photographer", "Airport pickup night arrival")

2. **[P2.1] Verify-before-book CTA eksplisit**
   - Komponen `components/VerifyBeforeBook.tsx` (lime accent, 1 blok)
   - Posisi: setelah Ijen Proof Rail, sebelum FAQ
   - CTA → `/verify-jvto` dengan section anchor relevan (legal/medical/police)

3. **[P2.2] Route Seriousness System UI**
   - Taxonomi baku di `lib/jvto-taxonomy.ts`: `RouteSeriousness = 'Easy' | 'Moderate' | 'Serious' | 'Expedition'`
   - Mapping dari field `physicality` yang sudah ada
   - UI chip dengan warna (green/amber/orange/red-ish) + tooltip; render di tour cards + package hero + tours hub filter

4. **[P2.3] Metadata untuk 10 halaman**
   - Tambah `export const metadata` atau `generateMetadata` di: `/contact`, `/destinations`, `/policy/*` (4), `/tours/from-surabaya`, `/tours/from-bali`, `/travel-guide/*` yang kurang

**Critical files:** `components/PackageAddOns.tsx` (baru), `components/VerifyBeforeBook.tsx` (baru), `components/RouteSeriousnessChip.tsx` (baru), `lib/jvto-taxonomy.ts` (baru), `app/tours/[slug]/page.tsx`, `app/contact/page.tsx`, `app/destinations/page.tsx`, `app/policy/*/page.tsx`, `app/tours/from-*/page.tsx`

---

### SPRINT D — "Cleanup & Guardrails" — 1 hari kerja

**Goal:** Maintainability naik, build reliable, repo bersih.

1. **[P3.1] TypeScript scope benar**
   - Balikkan `tsconfig.json` excludes ke hanya `node_modules`, `.next`, `out`
   - Kalau test files bikin TS error → fix testnya, jangan exclude
   - CI step `tsc --noEmit` wajib green

2. **[P3.2] Hapus Firebase dead code**
   - Hapus: `firebase.ts`, `firebase-applet-config.json`, `firebase-blueprint.json`, `firestore.rules`, devDep `firebase-tools`, dep `firebase`
   - Verifikasi: `grep -r "firebase" app/ components/ lib/` harus kosong setelah itu

3. **[P3.3] Hapus folder `Redisgn Website JVTO`** (kosong, typo, basi)

4. **[P3.4] Tambah tests kritis**
   - `api/tours/route.test.ts`, `api/volcano/route.test.ts` (mock MAGMA)
   - `components/InquiryForm.test.tsx` (validation)
   - `components/JsonLd.test.tsx` (snapshot TravelAgency + TouristTrip schemas)

5. **[P3.5] `loading.tsx` + `error.tsx`** di `app/tours/`, `app/destinations/`, `app/verify-jvto/`, `app/why-jvto/reviews/`

**Critical files:** `tsconfig.json`, `firebase.ts` (delete), `firebase-*.json` (delete), `package.json`, tests baru, file loading/error baru

---

### SPRINT E — "Phase D Foundation (CMS)" — 2–3 hari kerja

**Goal:** Mulai Phase D sesuai CLAUDE.md roadmap — tapi pragmatis.

- Evaluasi: Payload CMS vs. Sanity vs. Keystone untuk self-hosted on PostgreSQL yang sudah ada
- Rekomendasi awal: **Payload CMS** — jalan di PostgreSQL existing, admin UI bagus, TypeScript-native, deploy di same Next.js app
- Scope awal: Tours, Destinations, Reviews, Crew (bukan seluruh content)
- **Jangan** migrasi semua halaman ke CMS dulu — jalankan dual (static pages + CMS-backed listings) sampai authoring workflow terbukti

**Critical files:** `payload.config.ts` (baru), `app/(payload)/**` (baru), `collections/*.ts` (baru)

---

## 4. Success Criteria (Kapan Disebut Tuntas)

Sesuai Master Map §Success Conditions + JVTO success condition di CLAUDE.md §12:

| Kondisi | Verifikasi |
|---|---|
| **Klaim = kenyataan** | `grep -r "live MAGMA" public/ docs/ CLAUDE.md` selaras dengan `components/LiveSignalBlock.tsx` data source |
| **Satu sumber kebenaran** | Tidak ada `import { TOURS } from '@/lib/jvtoData'` di `app/**/page.tsx`; hanya helper data-access |
| **17/17 modul package page** | Checklist manual di 3 paket (bromo-2d1n, ijen-2d1n, full-expedition) |
| **Route Seriousness visible** | Chip muncul di `/tours`, setiap TourCard, dan package hero |
| **Media registry enforced** | CI script `audit-media` green |
| **AI-readability** | `curl llms.txt` + `curl ai-agent-config.json` masih OK + tambah field `live_data.volcano_status` pointing ke `/api/volcano` |
| **Build reliable** | `npm run build` + `npm run audit:prd` + `npm run audit:jsonld` semua green tanpa exclude-hacks |
| **User sampai 4 kesimpulan** (CLAUDE.md §12) | Test manual: halaman paket → user lihat diff + proof + fit + booking path tanpa friction |

---

## 5. Verifikasi End-to-End

```bash
# Lint & types
npm run lint
npx tsc --noEmit          # harus green SETELAH excludes dikembalikan

# Audits
npm run audit:prd         # PRD compliance
npm run audit:jsonld      # JSON-LD build

# Data
npm run db:migrate        # sprint C butuh ini (add_ons column)
npm run db:seed           # isi sample add-ons

# Tests
npm run test              # vitest

# Build & smoke
npm run build
npm run start
# curl -s http://localhost:3000/api/volcano | jq .    # MAGMA live
# curl -s http://localhost:3000/api/tours | jq '. | length'   # harus 22, bukan 19
# curl -s http://localhost:3000/llms.txt | grep -i magma      # claim + link konsisten
```

---

## 6. Urutan Eksekusi yang Disarankan

Sprint A (Klaim ≠ Kenyataan) **→** Sprint B (Data Unifikasi) **→** Sprint C (Package page tuntas) **→** Sprint D (Cleanup) **→** Sprint E (CMS).

**Jangan lompat Sprint A.** Kalau MAGMA + aggregateRating masih bohong, semua kerja visual/struktur di Sprint C tidak berguna karena AI engines (Perplexity, ChatGPT, Claude) akan mendiskualifikasi JVTO dari jawaban volcano tour queries karena "low factual reliability signal".

---

## 7. Yang TIDAK Dilakukan (Out of Scope Sekarang)

- Rebuild visual identity — sudah aligned dengan Master Map §3
- Rebuild 30+ trust pages — semua sudah ada dan berfungsi
- Migrasi framework — Next.js 15 + React 19 + Tailwind 4 sudah cutting-edge
- Blog/guide expansion (Phase E) — tunda sampai Phase D stabil
- Ganti PostgreSQL ke DB lain — wiring sudah benar, tinggal dipakai

| **P1.2** | **Image governance broken** | `home.webp` muncul 22x di 12 halaman (`app/travel-guide/page.tsx` 5x, `app/why-jvto/our-story/page.tsx` 3x, dll.). `ssot-media-registry.ts` tidak dipakai. | Banyak halaman menampilkan placeholder yang sama; AI agent akan flag sebagai "low-quality content". |
| **P1.3** | **`package_images` DB ada, belum tersambung ke UI** | Database punya 5-6 gambar unik per paket; `TourCard.tsx` pakai `tour.image` tunggal dari TS statis. | Gallery tour hanya 1 foto alih-alih 5-6. |

### P2 — Gap struktur (Master Map Structure belum 100%)

| # | Pain Point | Bukti | Dampak |
|---|---|---|---|
| **P2.1** | **Package page: 2/17 modul absen** | Tidak ada section **Add-ons** & **Verify-before-book CTA eksplisit** di `app/tours/[slug]/page.tsx` + `TourDetailClient.tsx` | Upsell hilang; jembatan ke `/verify-jvto` lemah di momen keputusan. |
| **P2.2** | **Route Seriousness System tidak eksplisit** | `app/tours/page.tsx` pakai `physicality` string bebas, bukan UI label berjenjang (Easy/Moderate/Serious/Expedition) | Discovery Cluster Master Map §3 tidak tercapai — user masih butuh baca copy untuk self-screen. |
| **P2.3** | **10 halaman tidak punya `generateMetadata`** | `/contact`, `/destinations`, `/policy/*`, `/tours/from-surabaya`, `/tours/from-bali` pakai metadata root template saja | Title/description generic → SEO & social preview buruk. |

### P3 — Technical debt yang menahan kecepatan

| # | Pain Point | Bukti | Dampak |
|---|---|---|---|
| **P3.1** | **TypeScript excludes diperluas** | Commits `7aadf51`, `6b4f8df` — test files + config files di-exclude dari TS build | Error test tidak terlihat saat build; regresi tidak ketangkap. |
| **P3.2** | **Firebase dead code** | `firebase.ts`, `firebase-*.json`, `firebase-tools` terinstall — **zero import** di kode page/component | Bundle size, confusing stack, security surface tidak perlu. |

## 3. Langkah Strategis (Roadmap Eksekusi, Urut Priority Fixed dari Master Map §7)

> **Metode:** Improve-in-Place. Jangan rebuild. Tutup gap dari P0 dulu (integritas klaim) → P1 (data truth) → P2 (UX struktur) → P3 (maintainability).

### SPRINT A — "No Lies" (Integritas Trust) — 1–2 hari kerja

**Goal:** Nol klaim bohong. Apa yang dibilang di CLAUDE.md, llms.txt, dan JSON-LD harus sama dengan yang di-render user/crawler.

1. **[P0.1] MAGMA live fetcher**
   - Buat `lib/magma.ts` — fetcher ke `https://magma.esdm.go.id/v1/...` (atau scrape resmi) untuk Bromo & Ijen
   - Buat `app/api/volcano/route.ts` — proxy + cache Redis (TTL 3600s, sudah ada `CACHE_TTL_VOLCANO` di `lib/cache.ts`)
   - `components/LiveSignalBlock.tsx` → async RSC atau client fetch ke `/api/volcano`; hapus array statis `VOLCANO_STATUSES`
   - **Kriteria selesai:** tampilan menunjukkan timestamp fetch nyata + data dari API; fallback graceful jika API down (tampil "Last verified: X, source: MAGMA" + link resmi)
   - **Jika MAGMA API bermasalah / tidak publik:** turunkan klaim di CLAUDE.md §7 & llms.txt menjadi "manual verification before each departure with reference link" — konsisten dengan kode

2. **[P0.2] Reviews aggregate dari DB**
   - Ganti `SITE_CONFIG.aggregateRating` statis → helper `getAggregateRating()` di `lib/siteConfig.ts` yang baca dari `/api/reviews/database` atau langsung `lib/db.ts`
   - `components/JsonLd.tsx` → AggregateRating schema baca dari helper ini di build/RSC time
   - **Kriteria selesai:** angka rating & jumlah review refleksi real-time dari PostgreSQL

3. **[P0.3] Exchange rates refresh**
   - Buat `scripts/refresh-exchange-rates.ts` + cron (via `scheduled-tasks` MCP atau Vercel cron) harian
   - Simpan ke Redis key `exchange_rates` + `exchange_rates_updated_at`; `siteConfig` helper baca dari cache
   - **Kriteria selesai:** harga konversi selalu < 24 jam umur

**Critical files:** `components/LiveSignalBlock.tsx`, `lib/siteConfig.ts`, `components/JsonLd.tsx`, `lib/cache.ts`, `app/api/volcano/route.ts` (baru), `lib/magma.ts` (baru)

---

### SPRINT B — "One Source of Truth" (Unifikasi Data) — 3–4 hari kerja

**Goal:** Halaman membaca dari PostgreSQL + API routes, bukan `lib/jvtoData.ts`. `jvtoData.ts` jadi fallback/seed saja.

1. **[P1.1] Migrate tour pages to API**
   - `app/tours/page.tsx`, `app/tours/[slug]/page.tsx`, `app/tours/from-surabaya/page.tsx`, `app/tours/from-bali/page.tsx` → ganti `import { TOURS } from '@/lib/jvtoData'` dengan `await fetch('/api/tours')` (RSC) atau helper `getTours()` di `lib/data-access.ts` yang baca DB langsung
   - `app/sitemap.ts` → baca dari DB supaya 22 packages (bukan 19) masuk
   - **Validation:** jalankan `npm run audit:prd` + bandingkan output dengan `/api/tours`; zero drift

2. **[P1.2] Enforce media registry**
   - Tambah util `lib/ssot-media-registry.ts::getHeroFor(zone, slug)` yang error di dev jika asset tidak terdaftar
   - Ganti semua 22 occurrence `home.webp` di `app/**/page.tsx` dengan panggilan registry
   - Tambah script `scripts/audit-media.ts` → CI gate yang fail kalau `home.webp` dipakai selain di `SITE_CONFIG.defaultHero`
   - **Kriteria selesai:** `grep -r "home.webp" app/` hanya muncul di registry, bukan di page code

3. **[P1.3] Wire package_images DB → gallery**
   - Perluas `/api/tours/[slug]` untuk return array `images[]` (sudah JSON agg di query SQL)
   - `components/TourCard.tsx` → pakai `images[0]` sebagai card; `TourDetailClient.tsx` gallery render semua 5-6 images
   - Migrasi Next `<Image>` dengan remotePatterns untuk `/uploads/` dan `/img/destinations/`

**Critical files:** `lib/data-access.ts` (baru), `app/tours/[slug]/page.tsx`, `components/TourCard.tsx`, `components/TourDetailClient.tsx`, `lib/ssot-media-registry.ts`, `next.config.mjs`, `scripts/audit-media.ts` (baru)

---

### SPRINT C — "Close the 17-Module Package Page" — 1–2 hari kerja

**Goal:** Halaman paket 100% modular sesuai Master Map §Package Page Structure Logic.

1. **[P2.1] Add-ons module**
   - Schema: tambah field `addOns: { id, label, priceIDR, description }[]` di `packages` DB (migration `npm run db:migrate`)
   - UI: `components/PackageAddOns.tsx` render checkbox + sub-total; posisi: setelah Payment summary, sebelum Closest Alternative
   - Seed data: isi untuk top-5 paket dulu (e.g., "Ijen medical clearance letter", "Extra photographer", "Airport pickup night arrival")

2. **[P2.1] Verify-before-book CTA eksplisit**
   - Komponen `components/VerifyBeforeBook.tsx` (lime accent, 1 blok)
   - Posisi: setelah Ijen Proof Rail, sebelum FAQ
   - CTA → `/verify-jvto` dengan section anchor relevan (legal/medical/police)

3. **[P2.2] Route Seriousness System UI**
   - Taxonomi baku di `lib/jvto-taxonomy.ts`: `RouteSeriousness = 'Easy' | 'Moderate' | 'Serious' | 'Expedition'`
   - Mapping dari field `physicality` yang sudah ada
   - UI chip dengan warna (green/amber/orange/red-ish) + tooltip; render di tour cards + package hero + tours hub filter

4. **[P2.3] Metadata untuk 10 halaman**
   - Tambah `export const metadata` atau `generateMetadata` di: `/contact`, `/destinations`, `/policy/*` (4), `/tours/from-surabaya`, `/tours/from-bali`, `/travel-guide/*` yang kurang

**Critical files:** `components/PackageAddOns.tsx` (baru), `components/VerifyBeforeBook.tsx` (baru), `components/RouteSeriousnessChip.tsx` (baru), `lib/jvto-taxonomy.ts` (baru), `app/tours/[slug]/page.tsx`, `app/contact/page.tsx`, `app/destinations/page.tsx`, `app/policy/*/page.tsx`, `app/tours/from-*/page.tsx`

---

### SPRINT D — "Cleanup & Guardrails" — 1 hari kerja

**Goal:** Maintainability naik, build reliable, repo bersih.

1. **[P3.1] TypeScript scope benar**
   - Balikkan `tsconfig.json` excludes ke hanya `node_modules`, `.next`, `out`
   - Kalau test files bikin TS error → fix testnya, jangan exclude
   - CI step `tsc --noEmit` wajib green

2. **[P3.2] Hapus Firebase dead code**
   - Hapus: `firebase.ts`, `firebase-applet-config.json`, `firebase-blueprint.json`, `firestore.rules`, devDep `firebase-tools`, dep `firebase`
   - Verifikasi: `grep -r "firebase" app/ components/ lib/` harus kosong setelah itu

3. **[P3.3] Hapus folder `Redisgn Website JVTO`** (kosong, typo, basi)

4. **[P3.4] Tambah tests kritis**
   - `api/tours/route.test.ts`, `api/volcano/route.test.ts` (mock MAGMA)
   - `components/InquiryForm.test.tsx` (validation)
   - `components/JsonLd.test.tsx` (snapshot TravelAgency + TouristTrip schemas)

5. **[P3.5] `loading.tsx` + `error.tsx`** di `app/tours/`, `app/destinations/`, `app/verify-jvto/`, `app/why-jvto/reviews/`

**Critical files:** `tsconfig.json`, `firebase.ts` (delete), `firebase-*.json` (delete), `package.json`, tests baru, file loading/error baru

---

### SPRINT E — "Phase D Foundation (CMS)" — 2–3 hari kerja

**Goal:** Mulai Phase D sesuai CLAUDE.md roadmap — tapi pragmatis.

- Evaluasi: Payload CMS vs. Sanity vs. Keystone untuk self-hosted on PostgreSQL yang sudah ada
- Rekomendasi awal: **Payload CMS** — jalan di PostgreSQL existing, admin UI bagus, TypeScript-native, deploy di same Next.js app
- Scope awal: Tours, Destinations, Reviews, Crew (bukan seluruh content)
- **Jangan** migrasi semua halaman ke CMS dulu — jalankan dual (static pages + CMS-backed listings) sampai authoring workflow terbukti

**Critical files:** `payload.config.ts` (baru), `app/(payload)/**` (baru), `collections/*.ts` (baru)

| **Satu sumber kebenaran** | Tidak ada `import { TOURS } from '@/lib/jvtoData'` di `app/**/page.tsx`; hanya helper data-access |
| **17/17 modul package page** | Checklist manual di 3 paket (bromo-2d1n, ijen-2d1n, full-expedition) |
| **Route Seriousness visible** | Chip muncul di `/tours`, setiap TourCard, dan package hero |
| **Media registry enforced** | CI script `audit-media` green |
| **AI-readability** | `curl llms.txt` + `curl ai-agent-config.json` masih OK + tambah field `live_data.volcano_status` pointing ke `/api/volcano` |

| **User sampai 4 kesimpulan** (CLAUDE.md §12) | Test manual: halaman paket → user lihat diff + proof + fit + booking path tanpa friction |

## 6. Urutan Eksekusi yang Disarankan

Sprint A (Klaim ≠ Kenyataan) **→** Sprint B (Data Unifikasi) **→** Sprint C (Package page tuntas) **→** Sprint D (Cleanup) **→** Sprint E (CMS).

**Jangan lompat Sprint A.** Kalau MAGMA + aggregateRating masih bohong, semua kerja visual/struktur di Sprint C tidak berguna karena AI engines (Perplexity, ChatGPT, Claude) akan mendiskualifikasi JVTO dari jawaban volcano tour queries karena "low factual reliability signal".

**Goal:** Halaman membaca dari PostgreSQL + API routes, bukan `lib/jvtoData.ts`. `jvtoData.ts` jadi fallback/seed saja.

1. **[P1.1] Migrate tour pages to API**
   - `app/tours/page.tsx`, `app/tours/[slug]/page.tsx`, `app/tours/from-surabaya/page.tsx`, `app/tours/from-bali/page.tsx` → ganti `import { TOURS } from '@/lib/jvtoData'` dengan `await fetch('/api/tours')` (RSC) atau helper `getTours()` di `lib/data-access.ts` yang baca DB langsung

### SPRINT C — "Close the 17-Module Package Page" — 1–2 hari kerja

1. **[P2.1] Add-ons module**

**Goal:** Maintainability naik, build reliable, repo bersih.

### SPRINT E — "Phase D Foundation (CMS)" — 2–3 hari kerja

**Goal:** Mulai Phase D sesuai CLAUDE.md roadmap — tapi pragmatis.

- Scope awal: Tours, Destinations, Reviews, Crew (bukan seluruh content)
